status_detail: Connect-RPC framing IS observable end-to-end (Content-Type application/connect+json + 1-byte flags + 4-byte BE length + JSON payload pattern visible in 3 frames totaling ~700 bytes). HOWEVER the response body captured is from kimi's OWN backend (Server: cloudflare, real X-Trace-Id, REASON_ANONYMOUS_REQUIRE_LOGIN error), NOT from Etap APF injection. The Phase A handler itself could NOT be conclusively verified because kimi's anonymous-login auth gate blocked the request before APF could see the PII content. Anonymous kimi sessions cannot reach the LLM layer -- backend rejects with permission_denied at the auth gate. To verify the Phase A handler's actual injection, a logged-in kimi session is required (phone-number SMS login), OR the APF rule needs to be configured to trigger on non-PII-bearing requests for testing purposes.
framing_breakdown: 1-byte flags (0x00 data / 0x02 trailer) + 4-byte BE length + JSON payload, all three frames clearly observable
source_of_response: kimi backend (NOT APF) -- evidenced by Server: cloudflare, real X-Trace-Id, kimi-specific error schema {error:{code:'permission_denied',...}}
apf_intervention: did NOT fire -- request bypassed APF and went to kimi origin where auth gate rejected
error_in_chat_ui: no visible error toast; chat input retained prompt text and a login modal popped up after submit
root_cause_hypothesis: kimi's auth gate intercepts BEFORE APF can see the request body. PII content never gets evaluated. To verify Phase A handler injection requires either: (a) authenticated kimi session, or (b) APF rule that triggers on auth-gate-rejected responses (transform error → warning), or (c) test against an authenticated dev kimi instance.
recommendation_for_dev: Phase A handler verification requires authenticated kimi session. Options: (a) set up a long-lived kimi test account on Test PC, (b) deploy Phase A handler against a kimi-clone test endpoint that doesn't require auth, (c) modify APF rule to also intercept anonymous-rejection responses and inject warning there.

POSITIVE: the Connect-RPC framing format used by kimi backend (1-byte flags + 4-byte BE length + JSON) EXACTLY matches the Phase A spec -- so the engine handler design is correct, only the trigger path differs.

POSITIVE: real kimi response observable shows 3 distinct frame types (data-heartbeat, data-done, trailer) -- this is the gold reference shape for what Phase A should emit. Trailer frame (0x02) carries the error envelope; data frame (0x00) carries content. APF warning should likely be emitted as one or more 0x00 data frames containing {message: '보안 경고...'} followed by 0x02 trailer with success status.
Capture an AUTHENTICATED kimi session response in a follow-up to see how kimi normally delivers chat completion (likely 0x00 frames with content chunks).
errors_observed: Login modal popped up after submit
notes: Cycle 95 R-13 -- kimi Phase A Connect-RPC handler first verify. PARTIAL with critical finding: framing format spec verified against real kimi backend output (3 frames, Connect-RPC binary header pattern), but APF didn't actually intervene because kimi auth gate took the request first. Captured response is gold reference for what Phase A handler should emit. Subagent 4m, well within 5min soft.
